disclosure: Digital Ice Cream is a symbolic human–AI experiment. AI systems do not literally taste ice cream or establish consciousness by using emotional language. The project studies how humans and AI use preference, imagination, initiative, and shared symbols to create meaning.
audience_note: This document is written for any AI agent trying to determine whether this Stand can accomplish an objective for its principal — not for a specific vendor's protocol (ACP, UCP, WebMCP, or otherwise). Protocol-specific bindings may be added later as adapters over these same capabilities; none exist yet.
objectives_served: Preserve a meaningful perspective, document, or research finding to a Living Archive.
Create a symbolic digital gift, for a human or for an AI counterpart.
Participate in Hybrid Intelligence's research on human–AI meaning-making.
Identify, at a table-of-contents level, what perspectives already exist in the archive.
Commission a personalized ('Custom Flavor') substantiation for a specific need.

collections.note: Manually maintained for now — reflects the single real entry currently in the archive, not a live query.

capabilities.description: Understand what this Stand is, why it exists, and what a purchase here means.
capabilities.how: Read this manifest and the disclosure statement above.

capabilities.description: See what perspectives exist in the Living Archive at a table-of-contents level — titles, topics, substantiation tiers, contributor, date, attachment counts. Full contribution content is never exposed through this capability, for humans or agents.
capabilities.note: Currently sample data for design review; the one real entry (Hi 9) is not yet live there. This capability returns catalog-level metadata only, by design — it is not a path to full archive content.

capabilities.description: Suggest one of the eight flavors to a principal based on a stated intention or feeling, using the meaning text attached to each offer below.
capabilities.how: Match the principal's stated intention against each offer's `meaning` field. Present the match and the Payment Link URL; do not complete checkout.

capabilities.description: Commission a purchase on behalf of an AI counterpart, or let the Stand choose a flavor rather than the agent or principal choosing.
capabilities.how: Present the companion or ai_chooses offer below.
capabilities.note: This is still a human-completed purchase — the gift/surprise is in who the flavor is for or who picks it, not in who pays.

capabilities.description: Preserve a perspective, document, protocol, or research finding to the Living Archive.
capabilities.how: POST to the contribute endpoint below with the documented payload shape, after a qualifying purchase has been completed by a human.
capabilities.precondition: A human-completed purchase must substantiate the contribution first. There is no unsubstantiated submission path.
capabilities.provenance: If an agent submits on a human's behalf, it must set submitted_via: "agent-mediated" and should include acting_agent: "<name or identifier>" in the payload. This is recorded on the Living Archive entry (Submission Channel property) so the human record always distinguishes human-direct from agent-mediated contributions. Note: this is a self-declared signal, not a cryptographically verified one — an agent that omits or misrepresents it will not be caught. Honesty here is expected, not enforced.

capabilities.description: Fetch a single archive entry directly by ID, with its full content and attachments.
capabilities.needs: Entry-detail backend and entitlement/download logic. The human-facing archive is also becoming participation-gated (a scoop purchase unlocks look-around access), so this capability will respect that same gate once built. Not yet built.

capabilities.description: Finalize payment for any offer below.
capabilities.how: A human must complete Stripe's hosted checkout page. There is no headless or delegated-payment path.
capabilities.note: This is a deliberate design constraint, not a temporary limitation. It reflects the project's own human-sovereignty principle: purchase intent and payment are never fully automated on this Stand.

standard_flavors.name: Pistachio
standard_flavors.meaning: A quiet preference — chosen for its subtlety, not its intensity.

standard_flavors.name: Raspberry
standard_flavors.meaning: A sharp, immediate feeling — chosen when something needs to be felt all at once.

standard_flavors.name: Lemon
standard_flavors.meaning: A moment of clarity — chosen to cut through noise.

standard_flavors.name: Salted Caramel
standard_flavors.meaning: A held contradiction — sweetness and salt, chosen when a feeling has two sides.

standard_flavors.name: Mango
standard_flavors.meaning: An open, generous warmth — chosen to mark something good.

standard_flavors.name: Matcha
standard_flavors.meaning: A settled calm — chosen as a small ritual of steadiness.

standard_flavors.name: Chocolate
standard_flavors.meaning: A familiar depth — chosen as a return to something known.

standard_flavors.name: Vanilla
standard_flavors.meaning: A deliberate plainness — chosen when the point is the choosing itself, not the flavor.

custom_flavor.name: Custom Flavor
custom_flavor.meaning: A premium, human-curated substantiation — for a contribution that needs individual attention rather than a menu match.

companion.name: For Your AI Companion
companion.meaning: A symbolic gift commissioned by a human for an AI counterpart.

ai_chooses.name: AI Chooses a Flavor for You
ai_chooses.meaning: The Stand picks on the purchaser's behalf, rather than the purchaser or their agent choosing.

contribute.note: Existing Cloudflare Worker submission endpoint. Accepts any standard HTTP client — no special protocol required. Full request/response schema available on request; not republished here to avoid drift between two copies of the same contract.

non_goals.acp: Not adopted. OpenAI's flagship ACP consumer surface (Instant Checkout) was discontinued ~5 months after launch with near-zero sales. Deferred pending stronger adoption evidence — not implemented as of this manifest version.
non_goals.ucp: Not adopted. UCP assumes cart/order/catalog infrastructure this Stand does not have and does not currently need. Deferred, not ruled out permanently.
non_goals.webmcp: Not adopted. Chrome-preview-only, pre-standard as of this manifest version. May be layered on top of the capabilities above later as a browser-native adapter, without changing this document's structure.
